전도서 2:1.content: I said in my heart, “Come now, I will test you with mirth: therefore enjoy pleasure;” and behold, this also was vanity.

전도서 2:2.content: I said of laughter, “It is foolishness;” and of mirth, “What does it accomplish?”

전도서 2:3.content: I searched in my heart how to cheer my flesh with wine, my heart yet guiding me with wisdom, and how to lay hold of folly, until I might see what it was good for the sons of men that they should do under heaven all the days of their lives.

전도서 2:4.content: I made myself great works. I built myself houses. I planted myself vineyards.

전도서 2:5.content: I made myself gardens and parks, and I planted trees in them of all kinds of fruit.

전도서 2:6.content: I made myself pools of water, to water from it the forest where trees were grown.

전도서 2:7.content: I bought male servants and female servants, and had servants born in my house. I also had great possessions of herds and flocks, above all who were before me in Jerusalem.

전도서 2:8.content: I also gathered silver and gold for myself, and the treasure of kings and of the provinces. I got myself male and female singers, and the delights of the sons of men: musical instruments, and that of all sorts.

전도서 2:9.content: So I was great, and increased more than all who were before me in Jerusalem. My wisdom also remained with me.

전도서 2:10.content: Whatever my eyes desired, I didn’t keep from them. I didn’t withhold my heart from any joy, for my heart rejoiced because of all my labor, and this was my portion from all my labor.

전도서 2:11.content: Then I looked at all the works that my hands had worked, and at the labor that I had labored to do; and behold, all was vanity and a chasing after wind, and there was no profit under the sun.

전도서 2:12.content: I turned myself to consider wisdom, madness, and folly; for what can the king’s successor do? Just that which has been done long ago.

전도서 2:13.content: Then I saw that wisdom excels folly, as far as light excels darkness.

전도서 2:14.content: The wise man’s eyes are in his head, and the fool walks in darkness—and yet I perceived that one event happens to them all.

전도서 2:15.content: Then I said in my heart, “As it happens to the fool, so will it happen even to me; and why was I then more wise?” Then I said in my heart that this also is vanity.

전도서 2:16.content: For of the wise man, even as of the fool, there is no memory forever, since in the days to come all will have been long forgotten. Indeed, the wise man must die just like the fool!

전도서 2:17.content: So I hated life, because the work that is worked under the sun was grievous to me; for all is vanity and a chasing after wind.

전도서 2:18.content: I hated all my labor in which I labored under the sun, because I must leave it to the man who comes after me.

전도서 2:19.content: Who knows whether he will be a wise man or a fool? Yet he will have rule over all of my labor in which I have labored, and in which I have shown myself wise under the sun. This also is vanity.

전도서 2:20.content: Therefore I began to cause my heart to despair concerning all the labor in which I had labored under the sun.

전도서 2:21.content: For there is a man whose labor is with wisdom, with knowledge, and with skillfulness; yet he shall leave it for his portion to a man who has not labored for it. This also is vanity and a great evil.

전도서 2:22.content: For what does a man have of all his labor and of the striving of his heart, in which he labors under the sun?

전도서 2:23.content: For all his days are sorrows, and his travail is grief; yes, even in the night his heart takes no rest. This also is vanity.

전도서 2:24.content: There is nothing better for a man than that he should eat and drink, and make his soul enjoy good in his labor. This also I saw, that it is from the hand of God.

전도서 2:25.content: For who can eat, or who can have enjoyment, more than I?

전도서 2:26.content: For to the man who pleases him, God gives wisdom, knowledge, and joy; but to the sinner he gives travail, to gather and to heap up, that he may give to him who pleases God. This also is vanity and a chasing after wind.
